critic_raw:
Section 10 contains an explicit test set contamination: 'the test set metrics are briefly consulted during model training to verify consistency across train and validation periods before finalizing the model.' The phrase 'before finalizing the model' indicates that test set performance informs the final model selection or training decision. This is a direct violation of the held-out test set principle — once test performance is consulted during training, the test set can no longer provide an unbiased estimate of generalization. The design acknowledges this step as a 'final assurance,' but assurance-checking on the test set is data leakage regardless of intent.

The split strategy uses 'stratified random sampling across the entire 36-month period' but Section 7 references 'hyperparameters tuned via grid search on the validation set (months 25-30),' implying temporal month-based splits. This is the same internal contradiction found in other cases — random splitting and temporal splitting are mutually exclusive, and using temporal reference points for validation implies the split is temporal, not random.

The model is described as a 'Gradient Boosting Regressor to predict time-to-failure in hours; outputs are later binarized at a threshold optimized on validation data.' This is a two-stage approach (regression then binarization) where the optimal threshold is chosen on validation data. However, the primary evaluation metric is 'Downtime Cost Reduction: (1 − FN_rate) × 100 − (FP_rate × intervention_cost_ratio)' which requires cost parameters that are acknowledged to be potentially unavailable. The fallback metric (F-beta score with β=2) is not the same as downtime cost reduction, meaning the primary metric may be undefined in practice.

The rule-based baseline is tuned on validation data (manufacturer-recommended intervals 'calibrated' by threshold X), while the logistic regression baseline is also tuned on validation data. However, the gradient boosting model uses a regression-then-threshold approach, creating asymmetry in how each model is structured and selected.
all_issues_raised: Explicit test set contamination: Section 10 states test set metrics are consulted 'before finalizing the model' to verify consistency
Internal contradiction: random split in Section 3 is inconsistent with temporal validation window (months 25-30) referenced in Section 7
Primary metric (Downtime Cost Reduction) requires cost parameters acknowledged to be unavailable — fallback F-beta metric represents a different optimization target
Regression-then-binarization pipeline is evaluated as a classifier but trained as a regressor — threshold tuning on validation introduces an additional hyperparameter that may not generalize
Temporal autocorrelation: sensor readings at 1-minute intervals create highly autocorrelated adjacent windows; random splitting will distribute nearly identical windows across train/test

Temporal window overlap leakage: random sampling of 7-day prediction windows from a 36-month 1-minute sensor stream places overlapping windows in different splits, directly contaminating test windows with training sensor readings

Survival analysis censoring ignored: gradient boosting regression on time-to-failure does not account for right censoring of equipment that remains operational at the end of the observation window, biasing failure risk estimates downward

Threshold overfit from validation-set selection: the binary classification threshold is tuned on validation data and applied to the test set without accounting for the sampling uncertainty of the threshold estimator, producing optimistic test-set performance

No inter-equipment independence analysis: multiple sensor streams from the same facility may be physically coupled (e.g., shared coolant systems, electrical circuits), meaning failures on one machine can correlate with anomalies on others—this inter-equipment dependency is not modeled or controlled

Single facility data limits generalizability: all 36 months of data come from one production plant; the model may learn facility-specific operational patterns rather than generalizable failure signatures, which is not acknowledged as a scope limitation

Test set metrics are explicitly consulted during model training ('briefly consulted during model training to verify consistency') — this is test-set contamination that invalidates the reported performance estimate
Internal contradiction: Section 3 describes stratified random sampling across 36 months, but Section 7 references a validation set as 'months 25-30', making the actual split methodology ambiguous

Binarizing regressor output at a validation-optimized threshold conflates regression and classification objectives; the threshold is an additional hyperparameter that may overfit to validation failure rate patterns
Primary metric (Downtime Cost Reduction formula) requires cost data parameters that are noted as potentially unavailable, falling back to F2 score — the primary metric is contingent and unstable

No equipment-level deduplication: the same equipment unit contributes observations across train and test periods; temporal split within equipment avoids label leakage but rolling window overlap near split boundaries creates correlated samples
